title: F014 — Delayed Choice Experiment

composition.colorLogic: cool scientific background, bright path lines, restrained accent colors
composition.labelPolicy: minimal labels, no dense paragraphs inside image

qaChecklist: Avoids forbidden objects and fake text
Does not overuse mystical or decorative elements

prompts.providerAgnostic:
Create a publication-quality scientific book figure titled "F014 — Delayed Choice Experiment".
Purpose: A source, possible paths, late measurement choice, and detector are mapped to show how the experimental arrangement affects what can be known.

Manuscript context: Delayed Choice Experiments Wheeler's delayed-choice experiments further challenged classical intuitions. In these experiments, choices made after a particle has apparently begun its journey seem to influence how the experiment manifests. Although these results do not imply backward causation in any simplistic sense, they demonstrate that the relationship between measurement and reality is more subtle than classical models suggest. The distinction between past and future becomes less rigid. Observation appears woven into the fabric of events themselves. F014 — Delayed Choice ExperimentA source, possible paths, late measurement choice, and detector are mapped to show how the experimental arrangement affects what can be known.F014-delayed-choice-experiment The Observer and Information The FHQCM proposes that observation should be understood through the broader framework of information. Observation is not merely seeing. Observation is interaction. Observation is relationship. Observation is the exchange and integration of information.

Scientific/conceptual concepts: delayed-choice measurement, observer-dependent experimental arrangement, observer participation.
Required visual objects: photon path, beam splitter or choice point, detector A, detector B, observer or measurement station, observer axis or observation node, information flow arrows.
Recommended short labels: choice point, detector, observer, information.

Composition: left-to-right experimental flow.
Camera/view: orthographic.
Visual hierarchy: source/emission → path or choice point → measurement/detector → observer/information interpretation.
Color logic: cool scientific background, bright path lines, restrained accent colors.
Label policy: minimal labels, no dense paragraphs inside image.

Avoid: fake citations, fake equations, unreadable text, watermarks, copyrighted characters, random mystical clutter, misleading scientific labels, random galaxies unless explicitly required, decorative clutter.

Make it clean, serious, elegant, uncluttered, and suitable for an academic manuscript.
